revision.comment: Bot: Creating normal ill redirect for [[Category:Lua-based templates]].
revision.text: #redirect[[:en:MediaWiki:Blockiptext]] [[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:pages created from Interlanguage links]] [[ja:qq]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]] to [[Category:qq]]{{Hidden category}}[[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text: #redirect[[:en:MediaWiki:Blockiptext]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]] [[Category:qq]]{{Hidden category}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages created from Interlanguage links]] [[ja:qq]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<noinclude>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude>"
revision.text: #redirect[[:en:MediaWiki:Blockiptext]] <noinclude>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude> [[Category:qq]]{{Hidden category}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages created from Interlanguage links]] [[ja:qq]]
#redirect[[:en:MediaWiki:Blockiptext]] <noinclude>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</noinclude> {{Hidden category}}[[Category:pages created from Interlanguage links]] [[ja:qq]]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Automatic wikipedia redirects

imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Bot: remove deleted category Pages created from Interlanguage links

revision.text:
{{Sensitive IP addresses}} You can use this form to block editing access from individual accounts or [[IP address]]es in accordance with the [[Wikipedia:Blocking policy|blocking policy]]. '''Please remember to include an informative reason for blocking.''' When using the pull-down menu, please also elaborate on the following line whenever helpful (citing relevant policy pages, [[Wikipedia:Sock puppetry|sock puppet]] accounts, the nature of the [[Wikipedia:Disruptive editing|disruption]], [[Wikipedia:Administrators' noticeboard/Arbitration enforcement|Arbitration Committee rulings]], etc.). Keep in mind that the block message will be very visible when someone tries to edit Wikipedia through the blocked account or IP. * When blocking legitimate '''bot accounts''', please ensure that you ''disable autoblocks'' to avoid blocking the bot owner. Related pages: [[Special:Log/block|Block log]], [[Special:Blocklist|List of active blocks and rangeblocks]], [[Special:AutoblockList|List of autoblocks]], [{{fullurl:Special:BlockList|action=unblock}} Unblock form], [[toolforge:ftools/general/ip-range-calc.html|Range block calculator]], [[Wikipedia:Template messages/User talk namespace#Blocks|List of block message templates]].
=== IP addresses === * '''In general:''' Most IP addresses should not be blocked for more than a few days, as they may be reassigned to innocent users. Only in ''extremely rare cases'' should IP addresses be blocked indefinitely. For detailed information on blocking IP addresses, please see [[Wikipedia:Blocking IP addresses]]. * '''Shared IPs:''' Before blocking an IP address with a long history of vandalism, please check if it is a shared [[proxy server]] or part of a dynamic IP address pool. **See [[Wikipedia:Blocking IP addresses#Shared IP addresses|here]] for details on how to identify and tag shared IP addresses. When blocking, please ensure that ''Apply block to logged-in users from this IP address'' is unchecked to limit [[collateral damage]] to innocent users, and consider reporting the IP via the [[:meta:XFF project|XFF project]]. * '''Blocks of governmental IP ranges:''' If you block ''any'' IP address in the ranges listed under the "Sensitive IP addresses" table on this page, you ''must'' notify the [[meta:Communications committee|Wikimedia Foundation Communications Committee]] immediately ('''contact instructions are [[:meta:Communications committee#Contacting the committee|here]]'''). These ranges are allocated to major governmental organizations; blocks of these organizations have political and public relations implications of which the Foundation's press relations team must be aware. Avoid long blocks of these IP addresses, and '''be especially careful in formulating your block messages''', because they will probably be seen and commented on by the press.
---- __NOTOC____NOEDITSECTION__

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: overwriting with templates included
revision.text:
{{Sensitive IP addresses}} You can use this form to block editing access from individual accounts or [[IP address]]es in accordance with the [[Wikipedia:Blocking policy|blocking policy]]. '''Please remember to include an informative reason for blocking.''' When using the pull-down menu, please also elaborate on the following line whenever helpful (citing relevant policy pages, [[Wikipedia:Sock puppetry|sock puppet]] accounts, the nature of the [[Wikipedia:Disruptive editing|disruption]], [[Wikipedia:Administrators' noticeboard/Arbitration enforcement|Arbitration Committee rulings]], etc.). Keep in mind that the block message will be very visible when someone tries to edit Wikipedia through the blocked account or IP. * When blocking legitimate '''bot accounts''', please ensure that you ''disable autoblocks'' to avoid blocking the bot owner. Related pages: [[Special:Log/block|Block log]], [[Special:Blocklist|List of active blocks and rangeblocks]], [[Special:AutoblockList|List of autoblocks]], [{{fullurl:Special:BlockList|action=unblock}} Unblock form], [[toolforge:ftools/general/ip-range-calc.html|Range block calculator]], [[Wikipedia:Template messages/User talk namespace#Blocks|List of block message templates]].
=== IP addresses === * '''In general:''' Most IP addresses should not be blocked for more than a few days, as they may be reassigned to innocent users. Only in ''extremely rare cases'' should IP addresses be blocked indefinitely. For detailed information on blocking IP addresses, please see [[Wikipedia:Blocking IP addresses]]. * '''Shared IPs:''' Before blocking an IP address with a long history of vandalism, please check if it is a shared [[proxy server]] or part of a dynamic IP address pool. **See [[Wikipedia:Blocking IP addresses#Shared IP addresses|here]] for details on how to identify and tag shared IP addresses. When blocking, please ensure that ''Apply block to logged-in users from this IP address'' is unchecked to limit [[collateral damage]] to innocent users, and consider reporting the IP via the [[:meta:XFF project|XFF project]]. * '''Blocks of governmental IP ranges:''' If you block ''any'' IP address in the ranges listed under the "Sensitive IP addresses" table on this page, you ''must'' notify the [[meta:Communications committee|Wikimedia Foundation Communications Committee]] immediately ('''contact instructions are [[:meta:Communications committee#Contacting the committee|here]]'''). These ranges are allocated to major governmental organizations; blocks of these organizations have political and public relations implications of which the Foundation's press relations team must be aware. Avoid long blocks of these IP addresses, and '''be especially careful in formulating your block messages''', because they will probably be seen and commented on by the press.
---- __NOTOC____NOEDITSECTION__
